carrier: Crum & Forster Segregated Portfolio Company (SPC) Captive
policyholder: ITI SP (Fairmont Specialty Trust, ITA Global Trust, Cayman Islands)
administrator: Seven Corners, Inc., 303 Congressional Blvd, Carmel IN 46032
notes: Certificate of Insurance (L1). Choice is the upgrade tier above Basic (FSG25-250506-01TM). Key upgrades: 90% out-of-network coinsurance (vs 80%), physiotherapy covered, higher acute onset limits, higher evacuation limits. Same carrier, same pre-existing definition, same claim rules. PPO network name NOT stated in certificate — NEEDS MANUAL REVIEW.
vs_basic_key_upgrades: Out-of-network coinsurance: 90% (vs 80% in Basic) → OOP max $1,000 vs $2,000
Acute onset cardiac/stroke age ≤69: $50,000 (vs $25,000 total in Basic)
Acute onset non-cardiac age ≤69: $75,000 (vs $25,000 total in Basic)
Acute onset age 70-79: cardiac $5,000 / other $7,500 (vs $2,500 total in Basic)
Extension of Benefits to Home Country: $10,000 (vs $5,000 in Basic)

evidence.conclusion: Identical to Basic: sudden, unexpected, Medical Emergency, during Period of Coverage after 7-day waiting period, treated within 24 hours, no Rx change in last 30 days, NOT a chronic condition with expected flare-ups.
evidence.section: Section 10 — Definitions — Acute Onset of Pre-Existing Condition(s)
evidence.exact_language: Acute Onset of Pre-Existing Condition(s): The occurrence of a Pre-Existing Condition that meets all the following criteria: a. It is sudden, unexpected, and occurs without advanced warning; b. It is a Medical Emergency; c. It occurs during the Period of Coverage, and after the 168-hour (7-day) waiting period; d. You obtained Treatment within twenty-four (24) hours of the occurrence; e. You did not have a change in prescription or Treatment related to the underlying Pre-Existing Condition within the last thirty (30) days; and f. Your Pre-Existing Condition is not Congenital, a previously diagnosed chronic condition with expected episodes or flare-ups, or a deteriorating condition which cannot be controlled and gradually intensifies over time.
evidence.flag: SAME AS BASIC — criterion (f) explicitly excludes chronic conditions with expected flare-ups, limiting cardiac/diabetic acute onset claims

evidence.conclusion: 36-month lookback — identical to Basic: diagnosed, treated, recommended, OR reasonable medical certainty within last 36 months. Includes chronic conditions and recurring complications.
evidence.exact_language: Pre-Existing Condition(s): Any Injury or Illness, including Mental Illness, which meets one or more of the following criteria prior to Your Effective Date: a. You were diagnosed; b. You received Treatment; c. Treatment was recommended to You; d. There is reasonable medical certainty that the Injury or Illness existed within the last thirty-six (36) months, whether or not previously manifested, symptomatic, known, diagnosed, treated, or disclosed.
